name: Tavily Search
description: Tavily AI web search. POST a query and get ranked web results with relevance scores; optionally an LLM-synthesized answer, raw page content, and images. Supports topic (general/news/finance), date/time filtering, and domain include/exclude lists.

usage.callShape: POST JSON {query (required, 1-1000 chars), max_results?, topic?, search_depth?, include_answer?, include_raw_content?, include_images?, time_range?, start_date?, end_date?, include_domains?[], exclude_domains?[]}. Only `query` is required.
usage.outputShape: results[].{url,title,content,score,raw_content}, answer, follow_up_questions, images[], query, response_time, request_id
quirks: Only `query` is required; all other fields optional.
answer is null by default — pass include_answer:true to get an AI-synthesized answer.
raw_content (per-result) is null by default — pass include_raw_content:true for full page text.
images[] is empty by default — pass include_images:true to populate.

On x402.tavily.com search_depth is fixed to 'advanced' (enum has only that value); the Orthogonal proxy exposes the full Tavily depth enum (advanced/basic/fast/ultra-fast) plus extra fields (chunks_per_source, country, auto_parameters, include_favicon, include_image_descriptions).
max_results accepts the string 'auto' or an integer 1-50.
x402.tavily.com uses x402 v2 (network eip155:8453); Orthogonal proxy uses x402 v1 (network 'base'). Same USDC asset, same $0.01.
content snippets are truncated with a [+N chars] marker in the default (non-raw) response.
usage.guide: Tavily AI web search. POST a query and get ranked web results with relevance scores; optionally an LLM-synthesized answer, raw page content, and images. Supports topic (general/news/finance), date/time filtering, and domain include/exclude lists. POST JSON {query (required, 1-1000 chars), max_results?, topic?, search_depth?, include_answer?, include_raw_content?, include_images?, time_range?, start_date?, end_date?, include_domains?[], exclude_domains?[]}. Only `query` is required. Output: results[].{url,title,content,score,raw_content}, answer, follow_up_questions, images[], query, response_time, request_id

name: Parallel Search
description: Parallel web search built for AI agents — high-quality structured results with excerpts. First-party parallelmpp.dev gateway, keyless pay-per-call x402 on Base (also supports MPP/Stripe upstream, but we use x402).

usage.callShape: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/search with JSON body {query, mode}
usage.outputShape: body.search_id; body.results[].{url,title,publish_date,excerpts[]}. excerpts hold the relevant text snippets.
quirks: Required field is `query`. Optional `mode`: 'one-shot' (comprehensive, default) or 'fast' (lower latency).
First-party parallelmpp.dev endpoint, keyless x402 (~$0.01 USDC on Base). Re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200, returned results[] with excerpts).
The gateway rotates the x402 payTo per request — the live 402 challenge is authoritative (payProvider re-probes at call time).
60 req/min per IP rate limit; handle 429 with Retry-After.
usage.guide: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/search with a JSON body {"query":"<search>", "mode":"one-shot"}. Keyless x402 (~$0.01 USDC on Base). The response is synchronous JSON: read body.results[] (each has url, title, publish_date, and excerpts[] of relevant text). Use 'fast' mode for lower latency.

name: Parallel Extract
description: Parallel content extraction — pull structured facts/excerpts from one or more URLs against an objective. First-party parallelmpp.dev gateway, keyless pay-per-call x402 on Base.

usage.callShape: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/extract with JSON body {urls:[...], objective}
usage.outputShape: body.extract_id; body.results[].{url,title,publish_date,excerpts[]}. excerpts hold the extracted content relevant to the objective.
quirks: Required field is `urls` (array). Optional `objective` (what to extract). Price is ~$0.01 per URL (minimum $0.01) — passing many urls scales the cost.
First-party parallelmpp.dev endpoint, keyless x402 on Base. Re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200).
The gateway rotates the x402 payTo per request — the live 402 challenge is authoritative.
usage.guide: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/extract with a JSON body {"urls":["<url>"],"objective":"<what to pull>"}. Keyless x402 (~$0.01 per URL on Base). The response is synchronous JSON: read body.results[].excerpts for the extracted content. Use this to pull facts from pages you already have URLs for.

name: Parallel Deep Research Task
description: Parallel deep async research task — submit a research objective, get a thorough multi-source report. Async (1-5+ min, poll for results). First-party parallelmpp.dev gateway, keyless pay-per-call x402 on Base.

usage.callShape: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/task with JSON body {input, processor}; returns {run_id}. Then poll GET https://parallelmpp.dev/api/task/{run_id} (free) until status==='completed'.

inputExample.input: Overview of the x402 payment protocol

usage.outputShape: Submit returns {run_id, status:'queued'}. Poll GET /api/task/{run_id} (free) → eventually {status:'completed', output/result:...}.
quirks: ASYNC: the POST only QUEUES the task (returns run_id, status 'queued'). You MUST poll GET https://parallelmpp.dev/api/task/{run_id} (FREE, no payment) until status==='completed' — can take 1-5+ minutes. Use exponential backoff (10s,20s,30s, cap 60s).
processor: 'pro' (~$0.10, re-verified 2026-06-10) or 'ultra' (~$0.30, deeper). Price is dynamic by processor.
Save the run_id — it is persistent, so results can be fetched later if you time out.
First-party parallelmpp.dev, keyless x402 on Base. payTo rotates per request (live 402 authoritative).
usage.guide: POST https://parallelmpp.dev/api/task with {"input":"<research objective>","processor":"pro"} (pro ~$0.10, ultra ~$0.30 for deeper). Keyless x402 on Base. It returns a run_id and queues the job — then poll GET https://parallelmpp.dev/api/task/{run_id} (free) until status is 'completed' to get the report (1-5+ min; back off your polling). Save the run_id so you can retrieve results later if needed.

name: Perplexity Search
description: Web search returning ranked results with snippets, powered by Perplexity.

quirks: Served via Sponge's x402 gateway (*.x402.paysponge.com) — a single x402 payment proxies the upstream provider. No API key. Base or Solana USDC.
usage.guide: Web search returning ranked results with snippets, powered by Perplexity. Served via Sponge's x402 gateway (*.x402.paysponge.com) — a single x402 payment proxies the upstream provider. No API key. Base or Solana USDC.

name: Perplexity Sonar Deep Research — async
description: Submit an async deep-research job (sonar-deep-research). Returns a job id; poll for the report.

quirks: Served via Sponge's x402 gateway (*.x402.paysponge.com) — a single x402 payment proxies the upstream provider. No API key. Base or Solana USDC.
Async: only sonar-deep-research is allowed; body must be { request: {model, messages} }. Submit returns a job id; result via poll.
model MUST be "sonar-deep-research" (the async endpoint rejects other models).
Body is wrapped: {"request":{model,messages}}. NOT a bare chat body.
⚠ Charges $0.01 even on a 400 (charge-before-validate) — get the shape right first.
Async: submit returns {id, status:"CREATED"}; poll x402 GET /v1/async/sonar/{id} (settles $0) until status="COMPLETED", result at body.response. Verified 2026-06-23.
usage.guide: Submit an async deep-research job (sonar-deep-research). Returns a job id; poll for the report. Served via Sponge's x402 gateway (*.x402.paysponge.com) — a single x402 payment proxies the upstream provider. No API key. Base or Solana USDC.

name: Exa
description: Exa neural + keyword web search built for AI — semantic search by meaning, returning ranked results (title/url/score) with optional page text. Defaults to the first-party api.exa.ai; also available via the BlockRun and StableEnrich aggregator gateways. Keyless pay-per-call x402. (For synthesized answers with citations, see Exa Answer.)

usage.callShape: POST https://api.exa.ai/search with JSON body {query} (optionally numResults, type:'neural'|'keyword'|'auto', category). Returns body.results[].

inputExample.query: latest research on AI agent memory

usage.outputShape: body.results[].{id,title,url,publishedDate,author,score}; body.requestId. (Add {"contents":{"text":true}} to also get body.results[].text page content.)
quirks: Required field is `query` (a search phrase or natural-language description). Optional: numResults (default ~10), type ('neural'|'keyword'|'auto'), category, includeDomains/excludeDomains, startPublishedDate.
This is the SEARCH endpoint → returns ranked results[] (id/title/url/score), NOT a synthesized answer. For a direct answer-with-citations use the Exa Answer service instead.

DEFAULT provider is the first-party api.exa.ai/search (~$0.007). Aggregator routes blockrun.ai/api/v1/exa/search and stableenrich.dev/api/exa/search (~$0.01) are also available — pin one with backendProviderId 'blockrun' or 'stableenrich'. All take {query} and return the same results[] shape. api.exa.ai re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200).
To fetch full page text in one call, pass {"contents":{"text":true}}; results[].text is then populated (may raise the price).
usage.guide: POST https://api.exa.ai/search (first-party, the default) with a JSON body {"query":"<search phrase>", "numResults":5}. It is keyless x402 (~$0.007 USDC on Base — no API key). The response is synchronous JSON: read body.results[] (each has title, url, publishedDate, score). To also pull page text, add {"contents":{"text":true}}. For a synthesized answer with citations instead of raw results, use the Exa Answer service.

name: Exa Find Similar
description: Exa find-similar — give a URL and get semantically similar pages from across the web. Served via the BlockRun and StableEnrich gateways. Keyless pay-per-call x402.

usage.outputShape: body.results[].{id,title,url,author,score}; body.requestId. Each result is a page similar to the input url, with a relevance score.
quirks: Required field is `url` (a single page URL) — NOT `query`. Returns pages semantically similar to that url.

No first-party api.exa.ai find-similar endpoint is published here, so this is served via the BlockRun (default) and StableEnrich aggregators (~$0.01). Pin with backendProviderId 'stableenrich' if desired.
Keyless x402 (~$0.01 USDC on Base). BlockRun route re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200, returned scored results[]).
usage.guide: POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/exa/find-similar with a JSON body {"url": "<a page url>"}. Keyless x402 (~$0.01 USDC on Base). The response is synchronous JSON: read body.results[] (each has title, url, and a similarity score). Use this to discover pages related to one you already have.

name: Exa Answer
description: Exa Answer — ask a natural-language question and get a synthesized, cited answer (not raw search results), powered by Exa neural search over the live web. Keyless pay-per-call x402 via the BlockRun and StableEnrich gateways.

inputExample.query: What is the capital of France?

usage.outputShape: body.answer (synthesized answer string with inline [n] citation markers); body.citations[].{title,url,publishedDate,image,favicon}; body.costDollars.total; body.requestId
quirks: Required field is `query` (a natural-language question). Returns a synthesized answer, NOT raw results — for ranked search results use the Exa Search service instead.
Answer text contains inline [1][2] citation markers that index into body.citations[]
No first-party api.exa.ai/answer endpoint is published, so this is served via the BlockRun (default) and StableEnrich aggregators (~$0.01). Pin with backendProviderId 'stableenrich' if desired.
Keyless x402 (~$0.01 USDC on Base). BlockRun route re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200, returned answer + citations).
usage.guide: POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/exa/answer with a JSON body {"query": "<your natural-language question>"}. Keyless x402, ~$0.01 USDC on Base. The response is synchronous: read body.answer for the synthesized answer (with inline [n] markers) and body.citations[] for the supporting sources (title + url). Use this for direct factual answers with citations rather than raw search results.

name: Exa Contents
description: Exa content retrieval — fetch the cleaned page text (and highlights/summary) for one or more URLs. Defaults to the first-party api.exa.ai; also via BlockRun and StableEnrich. Keyless pay-per-call x402.

usage.callShape: POST https://api.exa.ai/contents with JSON body {urls:["<url>",...], text:true} (or {ids:[...]}). Returns body.results[] with page text.
usage.outputShape: body.results[].{id,title,url,author,text}; body.requestId. text holds the cleaned page content. Optionally request highlights/summary too.
quirks: Required field is `urls` (an array of page URLs) — NOT `query`. You can also pass `ids` (Exa result ids). Pass text:true (and optionally highlights/summary:true) to choose what content comes back.
Pricing is per page per content type (~$0.001 each on first-party) — fetching many urls or multiple content types scales the cost.

DEFAULT provider is first-party api.exa.ai/contents (~$0.001). Aggregator routes blockrun.ai/api/v1/exa/contents and stableenrich.dev/api/exa/contents (~$0.002) also available via backendProviderId. api.exa.ai re-verified working 2026-06-10 (200, returned results[].text).
usage.guide: POST https://api.exa.ai/contents (first-party, the default) with a JSON body {"urls":["<page url>"],"text":true}. Keyless x402 (~$0.001 per page on Base). The response is synchronous JSON: read body.results[].text for the cleaned page content. Use this to pull the full text of pages you already have URLs for (e.g. from an Exa Search result).

name: Perplexica AI Search
description: Perplexica open-source AI answer engine — semantic web search with grounded, cited answers. Pay-per-call x402.

inputExample.query: What is the capital of France?

usage.outputShape: body.message (the grounded answer text with [n] citation markers), body.sources[] (array of source objects; may be empty)
usage.guide: POST a JSON body with a single "query" field (your natural-language question) to https://intel.asrai.me/search. Payment is x402 USDC on Base, ~$0.005 per call, settled automatically. The response is a plain sync JSON object: read body.message for the grounded answer (citation markers like [1] embedded inline) and body.sources[] for the cited URLs (note sources[] can come back empty even when the answer is good).

name: Honcho Agent Memory
description: Honcho is persistent memory + reasoning for AI agents: store every conversation turn, then query a peer's memory in natural language and get reasoned context back — not a similarity search over chunks. It continuously builds a representation of each user/peer (markdown summary + fact card) and can return LLM-ready session context. Keyless pay-per-call over x402: storing messages is $2/1M tokens ($0.001 floor), memory queries are $0.001–$0.50 by reasoning depth, and all reads (search, context, representations, cards, session/peer listing) are free with a SIWX wallet identity. Each wallet owns a private workspace.

usage.callShape: POST https://agentcash.honcho.dev/api/honcho/<op> with JSON body. Paid ops (messages, chat) use x402 on Base; all other ops are free with a SIWX wallet identity.

messages.content: I prefer concise answers and love hiking.

usage.outputShape: Per-op JSON — messages[] for store/search/list, body.content for chat, {representation} / {peer_card} / {messages,summary,...} for the reads.
quirks: One wallet = one private workspace. Paid endpoints derive the workspace from the PAYER wallet; SIWX endpoints from the SIGNING wallet. Use the same wallet for paid stores and SIWX reads so they share one workspace. Never send a workspace field.
Auto-provisioning: sessions and peers are created on first reference (by /messages or /sessions/peers/add). There is no explicit create call.

representation, card, and session summaries are generated ASYNCHRONOUSLY by Honcho's reasoning — they return empty string / null immediately after a store (verified) and fill in later. This is expected, not an error.
chat DOES reason over freshly-stored messages even before representation/card exist (verified: an accurate synthesis returned seconds after storing).
Paid ops advertise both x402 and MPP (Tempo); only the x402 route is used here. The 402 challenge is a standard, decodable x402 v2 payload (this resolved the earlier 'no decodable accepts' block).
usage.guide: Memory loop: (1) after each turn, POST /api/honcho/messages with {session, messages:[{peer,content}...]} — keyless x402 on Base, ~$0.001 for a short turn; it auto-creates the session and peers. (2) When personalization helps, POST /api/honcho/chat with {peer, query, reasoning_level} to get a reasoned answer from that peer's memory ($0.001–$0.50 by depth; 'low' $0.01 default). (3) Reads are free with a SIWX identity: /search (semantic), /context (LLM-ready bundle), /representation and /card (learned profile — async, may be empty at first), and the /sessions + /peers listings. Reuse one wallet so everything lands in the same private workspace.
